notes:
Removed the explicit container.setDepth(-100) call inside _renderTerrainLayer so the terrain container no longer renders behind the parchment rectangle. With no explicit depth, all elements share depth 0 and Phaser falls back to creation order: parchment (line ~73) -> terrain container (line ~80) -> title (line ~89) -> paths/nodes/labels/HUD. Net effect: terrain blobs now sit above parchment and below all foreground UI, which is the intended sandwich.

BEFORE (lines 225-235):
  225:    * scene (paths, nodes, labels). No per-frame work, no input listeners.
  226:    */
  227:   _renderTerrainLayer({ padX, padY, usableW, usableH, mapBounds }) {
  228:     const container = this.add.container(0, 0);
  229:     container.setDepth(-100); // strictly bottom layer
  230:     this._terrainContainer = container;
  231: 
  232:     const g = this.add.graphics();
  233: 
  234:     // Per-location blob: soft-edged gradient achieved by stacking three
  235:     // translucent fills of decreasing radius. Phaser's Graphics has no real

AFTER (lines 225-235, with the comment block expanded the file shifts by a few lines):
  225:    * scene (paths, nodes, labels). No per-frame work, no input listeners.
  226:    */
  227:   _renderTerrainLayer({ padX, padY, usableW, usableH, mapBounds }) {
  228:     const container = this.add.container(0, 0);
  229:     // No explicit depth - relying on creation order. The container is added
  230:     // in create() at line ~80, after the parchment rectangle (line ~73) and
  231:     // before the title (line ~89), so it naturally renders above parchment
  232:     // and below all foreground elements (title, paths, nodes, labels, HUD).
  233:     // Setting depth(-100) here previously hid the terrain BEHIND the
  234:     // parchment because parchment is at default depth 0.
  235:     this._terrainContainer = container;

Verification: line 228 (const container = this.add.container(0, 0);) unchanged; setDepth(-100) call removed; explanatory comment block in place; this._terrainContainer = container; unchanged; nothing else in the method modified. Other in-flight uncommitted edits in the file (terrain renderer work, audio-music swap) untouched. No new imports, no other depth values changed.
next_agent_suggestions: Visual smoke test: load the world map scene and confirm warm desert blobs are now visible under each location node, sitting above the parchment but below paths/nodes/labels/HUD.
